denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 02/08/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100126704          Group #: GRP-1016
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-106213
Claim #: CLM-2026-188849
Date of Service (requested): 01/19/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Summit Specialty Associates

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 01/21/2026 for Hip arthroscopy with labral repair related to Hip labral tear (M24.151). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Hip arthroscopy with labral repair
Diagnosis (as submitted): Hip labral tear (M24.151)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: CPB 0116 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
This request has been processed under a benefit category that does not provide coverage for the requested service (benefit category classification).
Our review applied the criteria in CPB 0116 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. The duration of Hip arthroscopy with labral repair exceeds plan limits without new clinical review. Continuation requires updated documentation not received.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with duration excessive. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with an Aetna medical director to review clinical information before you file a written appeal., they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100126704) and reference UM-2026-106213. 

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department

Standard criteria apply regardless of information previously sent to the plan.
clinical_context: The 42-year-old member has Hip labral tear (M24.151). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Hip arthroscopy with labral repair recommended as the next appropriate step. Positive anterior impingement tests; MRI labral tear with paralabral cyst. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that duration excessive bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Diagnosis, prior treatments, and insurer administrative posture reflect everyday commercial UM denials rather than textbook perfection.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Aetna correspondence with CPB and InterQual fingerprints and procedural tone.
